title: taraxacum subudum
intro: Taraxacum subudum, a lesser-known species of the Taraxacum genus, is commonly referred to as a type of dandelion. It is a flowering plant known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that has been shown to have potential health benefits. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including improved digestion and reduced risk of certain diseases. Taraxacum subudum can be prepared as an herbal tea or dried extract, and is often used in traditional medicine to support digestive health and alleviate various gastrointestinal issues.
